extra.reason: Set kubeflow-profiles/oauth2-proxy chart admin defaults to empty; externalized kiali root_domain to a variable wired from account module. grep clean, hclfmt+fmt clean.

Restricted ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy to cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy (mirrors dashboard/profiles); removed orphaned ingress.sa value. helm template + lint clean. metadata-grpc-service & service-cache-server kept allow-all (out-of-mesh callers); follow-up filed.

Replaced misleading inbound-cidrs=0.0.0.0/0 comment (grafana, kiali) and added matching accurate comment (dex, dashboard) across all four shared-ALB admin ingresses. Comment-only; value unchanged. terraform fmt clean on all four modules.

Wrapped exec in dynamic block gated on var.kube_data_auth_enabled in common/kube-provider.hcl and common/helm-provider.hcl (apply/flag=true keeps exec token-expiry fix; plan/flag=false drops exec -> kubeconfig fallback). Updated stale description naming the removed aws_eks_cluster_auth data source across all 12 variables.tf. terragrunt hclfmt clean; no aws_eks_cluster_auth references remain.

Moved route53 zone lookup into account module (data.aws_route53_zone.root + output root_zone_id); added mock root_zone_id; rewired dex/prometheus/kiali/kubeflow to consume var.root_zone_id from dependency.account.outputs.root_zone_id instead of their own live lookup. make plan-all now plans all modules cleanly: account does the single live lookup (Z0873790MAHSTW1NBQTC), consumers plan with mock zone_id (acm validation record known-after-apply). terraform fmt + terragrunt hclfmt clean.

All 3 workflows: added 'Install Terraform' step (hashicorp/setup-terraform@b9cd54a v3.1.2, terraform_version from env tf_version=1.9.8, wrapper off) after AWS creds; replaced tofu_version/tf_version action inputs with tf_path: terraform (satisfies action.yml mise-check and makes Terragrunt use the terraform binary; default tfpath is terraform anyway). env renamed tofu_version->tf_version. .tool-versions unchanged (still terraform 1.9.8). YAML validated. Not committed.

Migrated Terragrunt 0.86.2 CLI flags in Makefile (~18 lines) and 3 CI workflows: run-all <cmd> -> <cmd> --all; --terragrunt-non-interactive -> --non-interactive; --terragrunt-include-dir -> --queue-include-dir; --terragrunt-strict-include -> --queue-strict-include; hclfmt --terragrunt-check --terragrunt-diff -> hcl fmt --check --diff. Verified: make plan-all exits 0, 15 units planned, 0 flag/hard errors. YAML valid. Root terragrunt.hcl->root.hcl deferred to platform-amv.

Set TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled=false on the infra-validate-plan Plan step (was true) so the aws_eks_cluster data source stays gated off during greenfield plan, matching make plan-all. apply/destroy keep true (cluster exists then). YAML valid.

Disabled serviceMonitor on awslb-controller chart (removed the 2 set entries + orphaned monitoring_namespace var/input); added prometheus/charts/lb-controller-monitor (metrics Service in kube-system + ServiceMonitor labeled release=prometheus) wired via helm_release.lb_controller_monitor depends_on prometheus. Verified: original 'no matches for kind ServiceMonitor' error gone; awslb renders no ServiceMonitor; helm lint clean; isolated prometheus plan = 6 to add, 0 errors incl lb_controller_monitor; fmt clean.

Rewrote common/kube-provider.hcl + helm-provider.hcl to use var.cluster_endpoint + base64decode(var.cluster_certificate_authority_data) (from eks module outputs, mockable) + always exec; removed the aws_eks_cluster data source and kube_data_auth_enabled. Added cluster_endpoint/cluster_certificate_authority_data vars+inputs to 10 consumers; removed orphan flag var from ebs-csi/efs-csi; stripped TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled from Makefile + 3 workflows. Verified: make plan-all MAKE_EXIT=0, 0 cluster-unreachable errors, 0 hard errors (refreshes applied addons + plans new). fmt + YAML clean.

Added deploy-account/deploy-dex/destroy-account/destroy-dex targets (mirror deploy-prometheus, queue-strict-include) + wired into require-deploy-vars; verified dry-run renders correct terragrunt cmd and guard fires. Rewrote README: required-vars table, Route53 prerequisite, quickstart + corrected per-component order, new Account/Authentication(Dex)/Monitoring sections, *.admin.<domain> access + dex_admin_password login, kubeflow addons+deploy+dashboard URL, teardown, GH Actions vars/secrets, dashboard/ gitignore note. Verified: images exist, all 13 referenced make targets real, all 7 addon dirs exist, TOC anchors consistent.

Makefile: org_name ?= $(ORG_NAME) (reads env, no default); export ORG_NAME := $(shell ... lowercase) using := to avoid recursive-variable error; added require-org guard; require-deploy-vars: require-org and add-cluster: require-org. Verified: org via param and via ORG_NAME env both work (lowercased, no recursion); missing ORG_NAME fails fast on deploy-* and add-cluster; make -n parses clean. README: table now all-three-required + env-backed, export-once recommendation, bare make plan-all/deploy-all.

Live-patched ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy to allow cluster.local/ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway; verified gateway->/pipeline/apis returns HTTP 200 (was 403 'RBAC: access denied'). Made permanent in kubeflow/charts/pipelines: values.yaml ingress.sa=istio-ingressgateway; authorization_policies.yaml ml-pipeline-ui principal uses {{ .Values.ingress.namespace }}/sa/{{ .Values.ingress.sa }}. helm template matches live value; helm lint clean. Corrects platform-jk3 (oauth2-proxy was the wrong caller; gateway routes /pipeline directly to ml-pipeline-ui).

Verified: cluster is deliberately Istio ambient (istio/system/terraform: profile ambient); kubeflow/kubeflow-admin were mislabeled istio-injection=enabled instead of dataplane-mode=ambient, causing the broken enrollment. All 3 allow-all rules documented with ambient-aware justifications in pipelines/authorization_policies.yaml: service-cache-server permanently required (kube-apiserver webhook, out of mesh); minio-service + metadata-grpc-service justified now, actual scoping deferred to platform-f2i (blocked on platform-02x SA decision + platform-iyr ambient landing). Delivered & verified: Gateway API CRDs installed; istio-waypoint GatewayClass + waypoint Gateway for kubeflow-admin; ns-owner-access-waypoint AuthorizationPolicy enforced AT THE WAYPOINT (status Accepted, lowercase request.headers[x-forwarded-email] when-clause) — fixes the ztunnel L7-drop gap. FIXED & VALIDATED LIVE. Migrated profile-controller CompositeController->DecoratorController (+ sync.py object/attachments interface, + checksum annotation, pipelines chart 1.1.6). User deployed via terraform. Post-deploy: DecoratorController present, CompositeController pruned, webhook restarted with new sync.py. metacontroller: 0 reconciler errors (was 100% 'namespaces. not found'). Reconciliation confirmed: deleting ml-pipeline-ui-artifact auto-recreates it in ~4s at ghcr.io/kubeflow/kfp-frontend:2.16.1 (ready), now controller-owned. Both per-namespace pods (artifact+viz) managed at 2.16.1. Root cause was the v2.0.5->v4.11.22 metacontroller bump (during pipelines upgrade) without the matching upstream Composite->Decorator migration.

FIXED & VALIDATED on deployed cluster. Root cause: per-profile artifact proxy read mlpipeline-minio-artifact from namespace 'kubeflow' (default) instead of its own 'kubeflow-admin' because FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE was unset -> default-editor cross-ns 403 -> swallowed -> generic 'fetch provider secret' 500. Captured via patched 2nd frontend instance. Fix: added FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE: namespace to the artifact deployment in sync.py (matches upstream KFP), pipelines 1.1.7. After deploy + a DecoratorController re-sync, the artifact pod has FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE=kubeflow-admin and /artifacts/get returns HTTP 200 (seq + concurrent) with content. NOT ambient/version/CPU/waypoint (all red herrings). NOTE: existing profile namespaces pick up sync.py changes on the next DecoratorController resync (hourly) or a namespace event; I forced it via a namespace annotation.

RESOLVED (same root cause as platform-0vu). The metacontroller 'namespaces. not found' reconcile failure was fixed by migrating the profile-controller CompositeController->DecoratorController (metacontroller v4.x dropped core-resource CompositeController parents; KFP's own fix). Now confirmed the second half of acceptance: removed the manual stopgap kfp-launcher (label stopgap=platform-etk-manual) from kubeflow-admin and forced a DecoratorController re-sync -> the controller AUTO-PROVISIONED kfp-launcher in ~3s, now owned by the namespace (controller-managed, auto-heals), with correct content (defaultPipelineRoot=minio://mlpipeline/v2/artifacts, providers fromEnv:false + secretRef mlpipeline-minio-artifact, endpoint minio-service.kubeflow:9000). KFP_DEFAULT_PIPELINE_ROOT is set so the controller emits the kfp-launcher child. Per-namespace KFP provisioning is fully working.

COMPLETE & verified. Ambient dataplane labels are codified in IaC and live state matches (zero drift): (1) kubeflow ns -> terraform/main.tf:19 istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient, no istio-injection (with explanatory comment); (2) profile namespaces -> kubeflow-profiles profiles-namespace-labels-data configmap sets dataplane-mode=ambient + use-waypoint=waypoint, no istio-injection; the profile-controller setNamespaceLabels is additive so it won't re-add istio-injection. Live: kubeflow + kubeflow-admin both dataplane=ambient, injection empty. ml-pipeline-ui restrictive policy (oauth2-proxy principal, not allow-all) is in the chart and the pipelines UI loads (verified extensively this session). Fresh installs get correct labels via the admin-namespace.yaml pre-install hook; no manual kubectl label needed (the one-time istio-injection- removal this session was legacy-state cleanup). NOTE: the bead's original 2026-06-22/23 'dataplane split' notes were superseded by the xd4 waypoint work — profile namespaces now run ambient+waypoint, which is what's codified.

DONE & verified (chart + live). ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy is the restrictive oauth2-proxy-SA policy: chart authorization_policies.yaml -> action ALLOW, principal cluster.local/ns/{{ .Values.kubeflow.namespace }}/sa/oauth2-proxy, with corrected comment (oauth2-proxy reverse-proxies /pipeline/, not straight-from-gateway). Live deployed policy matches: principals=[cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy], selector app=ml-pipeline-ui, no allow-all rule (no regression). Pipelines UI loads without 'RBAC: access denied' — verified end-to-end this session. Codification caveat handled by platform-iyr (closed); blocked platform-nkx already closed.

No hardcoded gateway-SA principals remain.

DONE & verified live. Replaced the '- {}' allow-all in minio-service and metadata-grpc-service with scoped source principals; service-cache-server intentionally keeps allow-all (kube-apiserver webhook, out of mesh). EVIDENCE: ran live pipelines with ztunnel access logging (Telemetry API) -> the step-pod callers to BOTH minio-service and metadata-grpc-service are ns/<profile>/sa/default-editor (confirmed: kubeflow-admin/default-editor, 3x minio + 6x metadata). Persistent callers use pooled conns (didn't emit connection-complete), so combined the evidence with the chart's documented caller comments + existing policy principals. SCOPED LISTS: minio-service = ml-pipeline, ml-pipeline-ui, ml-pipeline-persistenceagent, ml-pipeline-visualizationserver, kubeflow-pipelines-cache + per-profile default-editor (range over .Values.profile_namespaces). metadata-grpc-service = ml-pipeline, kubeflow-pipelines-metadata-writer, kubeflow-pipelines-cache, ns/kubeflow/sa/default (metadata-envoy runs as default SA, fronts the external /ml_metadata route) + per-profile default-editor. VERIFIED: applied the scoped policies live, ran a pipeline -> Succeeded (artifact writes + metadata logging OK), artifact preview HTTP 200 (minio read by default-editor proxy), zero authz denials. Pipelines chart 1.1.7->1.1.8 + tf pin. Live policies are kubectl-applied (preview); a terraform apply of 1.1.8 reconciles them. RESIDUAL (minor): metadata's ns/kubeflow/sa/default is the loosest principal (metadata-envoy uses the default SA) — a future tightening could give metadata-envoy a dedicated SA.
